title: Containers in a pod without an infra container have different IP addresses
body: I observed that containers in a pod without an infra container have different IP addresses and hostname. Shouldnt containers in a single pod have the same IP and share namespaces among them, regardless of infra container not being present?

comments.body: containers without infra can't currently share namespaces. we originally planned to implement it, but it fell to the wayside
a lot of the pod infrastructure relies on the infra container, for better or worse.

I believe they can share namespaces, but it is not at all enforced, and
works like any other container doing --net=container - you have to manually
share individual namespaces. Pods in libpod are a very loose collection of
containers, and without an infra container to keep them together they don't
share much at all.

On Mon, Nov 18, 2019, 17:39 Peter Hunt <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> a lot of the pod infrastructure relies on the infra container, for better
> or worse.
>
> —

Ok, so sounds like this is mainly due to lack of cycles and not an inherent limitation. 
Containers in a pod are supposed to share network, ipc and uts namespaces all of which could be persisted without relying on any container being present.

"Supposed to" is a little strong... the original intent of a Pod in libpod was a loose group of containers that might or might not share containers. We've slowly drifted away from this, but I like keeping the convention that we don't force people to use an infra container and shared namespaces.
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
@mheon @haircommander Reading this, I am not sure if there are any action items?
Yeah it seems this limitation is a consequence of design
